title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Express5 Path Syntax
